circRNA_biology: Oncogenic circRNA (hsa_circ_0004087). In CRC promotes cell migration via Ezrin/AKT axis; in HER2+ breast cancer drives trastuzumab resistance by stabilizing GRB7 protein (PMID:34980129; PMID:34485151).
miRNA_biology: Tumor suppressor; p53-pathway effector (miR-34 family). Downregulated in glioma; overexpression inhibits glioma proliferation and invasion (PMID:24191262). Targets CDK6, BCL2, Notch-1.
Interaction_evidence: No direct experimental evidence for this specific pair. Computational prediction. Known circCDYL2 mechanisms involve protein binding (Ezrin, GRB7), not miRNA sponging.
Key_references: PMID:34980129 (Mol Cancer 2022); PMID:34485151 (Front Oncol 2021); PMID:24191262 (Mol Med Rep 2013)

circRNA_biology: Tumor suppressor circRNA (hsa_circ_0004872) in gastric cancer. Downregulated in GC; encodes MAPK1-109aa peptide that competitively binds MEK1, blocking ERK phosphorylation and suppressing GC proliferation/invasion (PMID:33836754). Also sponges miR-22-3p and miR-214-3p.
miRNA_biology: Poorly characterized miRNA (miRBase: MI0039721). No dedicated functional studies in cancer; listed in Human microRNA Disease Database but no established oncogenic/tumor-suppressive role.
Interaction_evidence: No direct experimental evidence. Computational prediction. Known miRNA sponge targets of circMAPK1 are miR-22-3p and miR-214-3p, not miR-12119.

circRNA_biology: Oncogenic circRNA from HUWE1 (E3 ubiquitin ligase, chrX). Upregulated in CRC (sponges miR-486, PMID:31998391) and DDP-resistant NSCLC (miR-34a-5p/TNFAIP8 axis, PMID:34917169).
miRNA_biology: Broadly-acting tumor suppressor (miR-29 family). Downregulated in papillary thyroid carcinoma (targets COL1A1/COL5A1), gastric cancer, and AML. Targets extracellular matrix and signaling proteins.
Interaction_evidence: Partial evidence: circHUWE1 sponges miR-29b/AKT3 in bovine myoblast development, validated by dual-luciferase assay and AGO2-RIP (PMID:32045877). However, not validated in a cancer context.
Key_references: PMID:31998391 (Front Oncol 2020); PMID:34917169 (Int J Genomics 2021); PMID:32045877 (Mol Ther Nucleic Acids 2020)

circRNA_biology: Uncharacterized circRNA from chr17q21.31 locus (MAPT/KANSL1 region), associated with neurodegenerative disease locus (tauopathies, PSP). No published cancer-related functional studies for this specific circRNA.
miRNA_biology: Oncogenic miRNA in breast cancer. Upregulated in lymph node metastasis-positive tumors; promotes proliferation, migration, and invasion by targeting TSPAN13. Correlates with large tumor size, advanced stage, and poor prognosis (PMID:30863082).
Interaction_evidence: No direct experimental evidence. Computational prediction. No published studies on this circRNA-miRNA pair.

circRNA_biology: circAPP (hsa_circ_0007556) derived from Amyloid Precursor Protein gene. Downregulated in Alzheimer's disease entorhinal cortex (PMID:36901741). A related APP-derived circRNA regulates microglial M1/M2 polarization via miR-1906/CLIC1 axis in AD mouse models (PMID:39953602). Biology primarily neurological, not oncological.
miRNA_biology: Poorly characterized miRNA (miRBase registered). No dedicated functional studies in cancer or neurological disease found. Minimal literature annotation.
Interaction_evidence: No direct experimental evidence. Computational prediction. Known validated miRNA partner of circAPP is miR-1906, not miR-5001-3p.

circRNA_biology: Tumor suppressor circRNA from NFIB (Nuclear Factor I B). In intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (ICC): competitively binds MEK1 to disrupt MEK1-ERK2 interaction (PMID:35841633). In breast cancer: encodes 56-aa peptide regulating phospholipase/arachidonic acid synthesis. In PDAC: inhibits lymphangiogenesis via miR-486-5p/PIK3R1/VEGF-C axis (PMID:32393487).
miRNA_biology: Context-dependent miRNA. Tumor suppressor in GC, NSCLC (targeting TFIIB-related factor 2), prostate cancer (AKT1), and pancreatic cancer (CCND2, enhances gemcitabine sensitivity, PMID:30021382). Oncogenic role reported in some cancer types via LATS2/CD44.
Interaction_evidence: No direct experimental evidence for this pair. Computational prediction. Published circNFIB mechanisms involve MEK1 protein binding and miR-486-5p axis, not miR-373-3p.
Key_references: PMID:35841633 (Mol Cancer 2022); PMID:32393487 (J Hematol Oncol 2020); PMID:30021382 (Biomed Pharmacother 2018)
